methods.description: This method describes the setup and execution of climate model simulations using the GENESIS model to study different climate scenarios. The simulations are used to understand the impact of different CO2 levels and ice sheet configurations on climate.

steps.description: GENESIS (version 3.0) has a spectral resolution of T31 (∼ 3.75° × 3.75°). The atmospheric model is coupled to 2° × 2° surface models including a non-dynamical 50 m slab diffusive mixed-layer ocean with dynamic sea ice and dynamic vegetation.
steps.searchable_summary: Set up GENESIS model with specified resolution and coupled surface models

steps.description: The applied palaeogeography comes from Herold et al. (2008), with dedicated modifications to Antarctica based on simulations by DeConto and Pollard (2003).
steps.searchable_summary: Apply palaeogeography and modifications to Antarctica in the model

steps.description: Different simulations were carried out as described in Burls et al. (2021). Here, we use simulation 1fumebi for the cold climate and simulation 3nomebi for the warm climate.
steps.searchable_summary: Select and execute specific climate simulations

steps.description: Simulation 1fumebi has a CO2 level of 280 ppm (1 × pre-industrial (PI)) and an AIS configuration with a full East Antarctic Ice Sheet (18.975 × 10^6 km^3 volume, area shown in Fig. S2).
steps.searchable_summary: Configure simulation 1fumebi with CO2 level and ice sheet configuration

steps.description: Simulation 3nomebi is forced by a CO2 level of 840 ppm (3 × PI) with no ice cover.
steps.searchable_summary: Configure simulation 3nomebi with CO2 level and no ice cover

steps.description: Both simulations use present-day orbital settings.
steps.searchable_summary: Set present-day orbital settings for simulations

steps.description: The vegetation on non-glaciated land, which has an influence on the regional climate, is determined by the dynamic vegetation model BIOME4.
steps.searchable_summary: Use BIOME4 model to determine vegetation on non-glaciated land
